sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: DHI GROUP, INC. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: DHI Group, Inc. has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, revealing a challenging financial year with a 10% decrease in total revenues to $127.8 million and a net loss of $13.5 million. The company attributes these results primarily to macroeconomic conditions impacting its Dice segment, though ClearanceJobs saw a 1% revenue increase. DHI Group is focusing on strategic restructuring, product innovation, and capital management, including stock repurchases, to navigate market risks and capitalize on future growth opportunities in the technology and government sectors.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) $375 Million Power Generation Lease Facility
feed.summary: Atlas Energy Solutions (AESI) secured a $375 million lease facility from Eldridge Capital Management for new power generation assets, building on its acquisition of Moser Energy Systems. This facility is crucial for its integrated power generation platform, but adds financial complexity given its current unprofitability and weak interest coverage. Investors have diverse views on the stock's fair value, highlighting a need to balance potential upsides with financing risks.

feed.title: Why Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile Are Beating the Market in 2026
feed.summary: Verizon, AT&T, and T-Mobile are outperforming the S&P 500 in early 2026, primarily due to strong earnings reports rather than overall sector gains. Verizon saw a significant boost from subscriber additions, AT&T's convergence strategy is driving growth, and T-Mobile impressed with updated long-term outlooks despite some quarterly misses. All three telecom giants provided reasons for investor optimism, with Verizon's turnaround being particularly highlighted.

feed.title: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) Director Sells $151,755.00 in Stock
feed.summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) Director Paul Edward Chamberlain sold 1,500 shares of the company's stock for $151,755.00 on February 12th, reducing his ownership by 3.13%. This follows another sale of 1,500 shares in November. The article also provides details on ServiceNow's recent earnings, stock performance, analyst ratings, and institutional trading activity.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) COO Panagiotis Tsourapas Sells 15,000 Shares
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive's COO, Panagiotis Tsourapas, sold 15,000 shares of company stock for over $1.46 million on February 12th, reducing his holdings by 59.02%. This follows another sale of 35,000 shares on February 11th. The company recently reported better-than-expected earnings and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.52 per share.

feed.title: AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) Insider Sells $540,000.00 in Stock
feed.summary: AnaptysBio (NASDAQ:ANAB) insider Eric Loumeau sold 10,000 shares of the company's stock for $540,000 on February 11th, reducing his ownership by 43.79%. Following this transaction, Loumeau now owns 12,835 shares valued at approximately $693,090. The biotechnology company also recently announced a $100 million stock repurchase plan, indicating management believes the stock is undervalued.

feed.title: American Express (NYSE:AXP) Insider Sells $2,820,301.82 in Stock
feed.summary: American Express (NYSE:AXP) insider Howard Grosfield recently sold 8,134 shares of the company's stock for over $2.8 million, reducing his position by 46.30%. The sale occurred on February 12th at an average price of $346.73 per share. This transaction, disclosed in an SEC filing, reduced Grosfield's total holdings to 9,433 shares valued at approximately $3.27 million.

feed.title: Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) CTO Sells $378,000.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) CTO Robert Blumofe sold 3,500 shares of company stock for $378,000 at an average price of $108.00 per share on February 13th, 2026. This transaction reduced his direct ownership by 19.36% to 14,574 shares. The article also notes a prior sale in January and discusses the stock's recent performance, institutional holdings, and analyst ratings.

feed.title: Leidos declares quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per share
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:LDOS) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 16, 2026. The company has a 14-year history of dividend payments and has increased its dividend for the past 7 consecutive years. Leidos, a technology and industry services provider for government and commercial customers, recently reported annual revenues of approximately $16.7 billion and is actively expanding its business through acquisitions and strategic partnerships in AI governance and federal agency operations.

feed.title: Honeywell declares quarterly dividend of $1.19 per share
feed.summary: Honeywell (NASDAQ:HON) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.19 per share, payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record by February 27, 2026. This decision reflects the company's commitment to shareholder returns, marking its 41st consecutive year of dividend payments and 15th consecutive year of dividend increases. The announcement follows a strong financial performance in Q4 2025, where Honeywell exceeded analyst expectations for both earnings per share and revenue.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets declares first-quarter 2026 dividend of $0.72
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets, Inc. (CBOE) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.72 per share for the first quarter of 2026, payable on March 13, 2026. The company has a strong dividend history, maintaining payments for 16 consecutive years and raising them for 11 straight years. This announcement follows their solid fourth-quarter 2025 earnings which exceeded analyst expectations, though the stock saw a pre-market decline.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials raises quarterly dividend by 6% to $0.52 per share
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company announced a 6% increase in its quarterly cash dividend to $0.52 per share, payable on March 23, 2026, marking its twelfth consecutive year of dividend increases. The company, a leading producer of construction aggregates, attributes this to strong operational and financial performance, including a 6.54% revenue growth, and an impressive 55-year streak of maintaining dividend payments. This dividend hike aligns with its commitment to delivering long-term value to shareholders.

feed.title: Former Joint Chiefs vice chairman joins Northrop Grumman board
feed.summary: Northrop Grumman Corporation has elected retired Admiral Christopher Grady, former vice chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, to its board of directors. Grady's extensive military background and leadership experience are expected to provide valuable insights to the aerospace and defense company. Northrop Grumman stock has seen significant growth over the past year, trading near its 52-week high, and the company has strong financial fundamentals, maintaining dividend payments for 56 consecutive years.

feed.title: X3 Holdings seeks Nasdaq hearing after delisting notice over $1 rule
feed.summary: X3 Holdings (Nasdaq: XTKG) faces delisting from Nasdaq due to its stock closing below $1 for 32 consecutive business days. The company's recent one-for-six reverse stock split made it ineligible for the standard 180-day compliance period. XTKG has requested a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel to appeal the decision and temporarily stay the suspension, though there's no guarantee of success.

feed.title: X3 Holdings to consolidate shares; small investors paid for fractions
feed.summary: X3 Holdings (Nasdaq: XTKG) announced that its shareholders approved a share consolidation plan at its Annual General Meeting on February 9, 2026. This measure is intended to help the company regain compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule. Fractional shares resulting from the consolidation will be redeemed for cash at the post-consolidation closing price.

feed.title: EIX Downgraded by UBS with New Price Target of $78 | EIX Stock N
feed.summary: UBS has downgraded Edison International (EIX) from a "Buy" to a "Neutral" rating, although it concurrently increased the price target from $70.00 to $78.00. This adjustment reflects an improved valuation outlook despite the rating downgrade. The article also provides a summary of recent analyst ratings and price targets from other firms, alongside the average Wall Street analyst forecast and GuruFocus' estimated fair value.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) Launches New Medium-Term Note Programs
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company (WFC) has launched new Medium-Term Note Programs, Series AA and Series B, to enhance its financial flexibility and funding capabilities. This strategic move, reported on February 13, 2026, involves filings with the SEC for Distribution Agreements and global master notes. The initiative aims to optimize Wells Fargo's capital structure and support its growth strategies in an evolving financial landscape.

feed.title: SAP.DE SAP SE (XETRA) €171.72 13 Feb 2026: AI cloud momentum may raise valuation
feed.summary: SAP.DE stock closed at €171.72 on XETRA, with a gain of 1.19% on February 13, 2026, driven by enterprise AI adoption in SAP's cloud stack and a return to large-cap tech. Despite a current P/E of 27.52, the article suggests potential for re-rating if AI-led cloud revenue accelerates. Meyka AI rates SAP.DE as a "BUY" with a score of 74.26/B+ and forecasts a yearly target of €286.02, indicating a 66.50% upside, although short-term forecasts suggest possible pullbacks.

feed.title: BlackRock Canada Closes the Market
feed.summary: The BlackRock Canada team, alongside members of their Families & Allies at BlackRock (FAB) Network, participated in closing the market at the Toronto Stock Exchange to celebrate Family Day. The event highlighted learning, financial inclusion, and introduced family members to Canada's financial markets. BlackRock is a global asset manager focused on helping individuals achieve financial well-being through investment, advisory, and risk management solutions.

feed.title: Align Technology Inc. Is Quietly Printing Money – But Is This Invisible Braces King Still Worth Yo
feed.summary: Align Technology, known for its Invisalign clear aligners, is a multibillion-dollar company often in the spotlight due to its volatile stock performance. The article explores whether the company, which merges health, beauty, and tech, is still a worthwhile investment given its premium product, advanced digital dentistry technology, and significant competition. While the product remains a "game-changer" for consumers, the stock is considered high-risk with potential for good returns on dips for investors who can tolerate volatility.

feed.title: Pool Corporation Announces Director Carlos Sabater’s Planned Exit
feed.summary: Pool Corporation announced that director Carlos A. Sabater will not seek re-election after his term concludes at the company's 2026 annual meeting of shareholders. His departure is attributed to an orderly governance transition and is not due to any disagreements over company operations or policies. Analysts currently rate POOL stock as a "Hold" with a price target of $275.00, and TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark views POOL as "Neutral" due to financial stability improvements and revenue growth balanced by margin compression and a long-term downtrend.

feed.title: Most CFOs expect larger IT budgets, ‘collapsing’ staff growth: Gartner
feed.summary: A Gartner survey reveals that three-quarters of global CFOs anticipate increased technology budgets this year, with nearly half expecting rises of 10% or more, driven by digital transformation, AI adoption, and cybersecurity needs. Conversely, staff growth expectations are significantly shrinking from 6% in 2025 to 2% in 2026, indicating a shift towards optimization through automation and AI rather than headcount expansion. While overall IT spending is projected to reach $6.15 trillion in 2026, AI investment within finance tech budgets remains modest for most CFOs, though a decisive acceleration in AI spending is anticipated.

feed.title: The State of Employee Benefits: Navigating Costs, Compliance, and Innovation
feed.summary: This article announces an upcoming webinar by CBIZ Employee Benefits focusing on managing escalating healthcare costs, navigating complex regulations, and implementing innovative benefits strategies. The discussion aims to help organizations maintain competitive, compliant, and cost-effective benefits while ensuring employee well-being. Topics covered will include strategies for healthcare spend, evolving regulatory requirements, pharmacy benefit strategies, and maximizing tax-advantaged benefits.

feed.title: West Virginia plans new gas plant, solar farms for 500,000 homes
feed.summary: Mon Power and Potomac Edison, subsidiaries of FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE), have selected a 35-acre site in Maidsville, West Virginia for a new 1,200-megawatt natural gas power plant. This plant is expected to power approximately half a million homes and, if approved by 2027, could be online by late 2031. The companies are also seeking approval for 70 megawatts of new solar generation on reclaimed lands, complementing 30 megawatts already built since 2024, as part of an Integrated Resource Plan to enhance reliability and affordability.

feed.title: A Look At WEC Energy Group (WEC) Valuation After Earnings, Guidance Reaffirmation And Tariff Uncertainty
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group (WEC) is currently valued by Simply Wall St as 5.8% undervalued at $114.03 per share, with a fair value estimate of $121.03. This valuation follows its latest earnings report which showed higher sales but lower net income, alongside reaffirmed 2026 earnings guidance and long-term growth targets. The company's significant infrastructure modernization plans, including $28 billion in capital expenditure over five years, are expected to drive predictable earnings growth, but regulatory decisions and potential equity dilution pose risks to this outlook.

feed.title: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) Valuation After 2025 Earnings Strength And 2026 Guidance For CF And Gene Editing Growth
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) recently released strong full-year 2025 results and issued positive 2026 revenue guidance, driven by its cystic fibrosis franchise and new gene editing products like CASGEVY, JOURNAVX, and ALYFTREK. The company's stock has shown momentum, with a 90-day return of 6.38% and a five-year return of 121.62%. Despite trading below the average analyst price target and an intrinsic value estimate of $504, its current P/E ratio of 32.1x suggests the market is already pricing in significant future earnings growth.

feed.title: Is It Time To Reconsider NetApp (NTAP) After Recent Share Price Weakness?
feed.summary: This article analyzes NetApp (NTAP) following recent share price declines, concluding that the stock appears undervalued. Using Discounted Cash Flow and Price-to-Earnings ratio analyses, Simply Wall St suggests NetApp is trading below its intrinsic value. The article encourages investors to consider their own narratives and assumptions regarding NetApp's future potential in AI and hybrid cloud solutions.

feed.title: Ariz. Uber Verdict Has Implications Beyond Ride-Hailing Cos.
feed.summary: The article discusses the implications of a recent federal jury verdict in Arizona against Uber Technologies Inc., where the company was ordered to pay $8.5 million to a woman sexually assaulted by her driver. While Uber declared a partial victory, the case highlights broader issues for ride-hailing companies and potentially other businesses regarding liability for contractor conduct. The full content requires a Law360 subscription.

feed.title: Supreme Court to Hear Intel 401(k) Complaint Next Term
feed.summary: The Supreme Court has agreed to delay hearing a complaint against Intel Corp. regarding allegedly imprudent investment options in its 401(k) plans until its next term, starting in October. Both parties requested more time to submit documents. The case will review the "meaningful benchmark" rule, which requires plaintiffs to identify comparable investments when alleging mismanagement of retirement plan assets, a rule the Intel plaintiffs argue is inconsistently applied and overly strict.

feed.title: Emerson Establishes New 364-Day $2 Billion Credit Facility
feed.summary: Emerson Electric Co. secured a new $2 billion 364-day unsecured credit facility on February 10, 2026, replacing a previous $3 billion agreement. This facility, which runs until February 9, 2027, aims to provide liquidity for general corporate purposes and act as a backstop for the company's commercial paper program. Currently, Emerson has no outstanding loans under this facility and does not intend to borrow, emphasizing its role in maintaining flexible, standby liquidity.

feed.title: PG&E outlook raised to positive by Moody’s on financial improvement
feed.summary: Moody's Ratings has upgraded PG&E Corporation and Pacific Gas & Electric Company's outlook from stable to positive, affirming their current ratings on approximately $44 billion of debt securities. This improved outlook is based on the expectation that both entities will enhance their financial ratios during 2026-2027, driven by cost-saving measures, a scheduled rate increase, and continued progress in wildfire mitigation efforts. The ratings affirmation also considers PG&E's constructive regulatory relationship and access to the state's wildfire fund, balanced against ongoing wildfire exposure.

feed.title: Advantage Solutions downgraded by S&P amid exchange offer uncertainty
feed.summary: S&P Global Ratings has downgraded Advantage Solutions Inc. and placed its ratings on CreditWatch negative due to uncertainty surrounding the success and implementation of the company’s recent exchange offer. The proposed exchange, supported by a majority of debt holders, involves a $1.1 billion term loan and $595 million senior secured notes, offering new super priority debt and cash in exchange for existing holdings. The downgrade also reflects challenging industry conditions, including revenue and EBITDA declines expected in 2025 and 2026, though reorganization efforts and the exchange offer, if completed, could moderately reduce leverage despite increased interest expenses.

feed.title: Cboe Global Markets Declares First-Quarter 2026 Dividend
feed.summary: Cboe Global Markets Inc. announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.72 per share of common stock for the first quarter of 2026. The dividend is payable on March 13, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 27, 2026. Cboe Global Markets operates a leading derivatives and securities exchange network providing trading solutions in multiple asset classes.

feed.title: NRG Energy Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: NRG Energy Inc. (NRG) stock rose by 6.52% to $172.35, outperforming the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average on a strong trading day. This marked the stock's seventh consecutive day of gains. The article highlights NRG Energy Inc.'s positive performance relative to broader market indices.

feed.title: Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Gartner, HP, Globalstar, Amphenol, and Equifax rose following a softer-than-anticipated Consumer Price Index report, which has increased investor optimism for interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve. The CPI report showed prices rising 0.2% from December to January and annual inflation moderating to 2.4%, both below forecasts. This news prompted a rally in stocks, particularly for smaller companies, as lower interest rates are expected to stimulate economic activity.

feed.title: SPS Commerce (SPSC) Q4 Earnings Beat Estimates
feed.summary: SPS Commerce (SPSC) reported Q4 earnings of $1.14 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1 per share and exceeding last year's $0.89. Despite the earnings beat, the company's Q4 revenues of $192.65 million missed the consensus estimate by 0.45%. SPS Commerce shares have lost 23.1% year-to-date, and the stock currently holds a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).

feed.title: |【即時新聞】YieldMax ABNB選擇權收益策略ETF宣布每股0.2213美元週配息，除息日程一次掌握
feed.summary: |YieldMax ABNB選擇權收益策略ETF (ABNY) 宣布將配發每股0.2213美元的週配息，除息日和股東名冊基準日均設定為2月5日，股利發放日為2月6日。此公告為關注該ETF短期收益的投資人提供了關鍵數據，並詳述了ABNY的最新收盤行情和交易量變化。

feed.title: SCE Extends More Than 500 Offers for Eaton Fire Recovery in Just Over 100 Days
feed.summary: Southern California Edison (SCE) has reached a major milestone in its Wildfire Recovery Compensation Program, extending over 500 compensation offers totaling more than $165 million to individuals and businesses affected by the Eaton Fire. The program aims to provide streamlined and faster compensation than traditional litigation. SCE encourages claimants to gather documents, check emails for updates, and respond promptly to information requests to accelerate the process.

feed.title: Cencora raises $3B to repay 364-day credit for OneOncology acquisition
feed.summary: Cencora, Inc. has successfully closed a $3.0 billion senior notes offering, consisting of five tranches with varying interest rates and maturity dates ranging from 2029 to 2056. The net proceeds from this offering will primarily be used to repay its 364-Day Term Credit Agreement, which was obtained to finance a portion of its acquisition of OneOncology, with any remaining funds allocated for general corporate purposes. The offering was an underwritten registered public offering managed by several major financial institutions.

feed.title: W. R. Berkley Corporation Declares Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation announced a regular quarterly cash dividend of 9 cents per share on its common stock. The dividend will be paid on March 4, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 23, 2026. The company is an insurance holding company and a major commercial lines writer in the U.S.

feed.title: Insurer W. R. Berkley to pay 9¢ a share dividend on Mar. 4
feed.summary: W. R. Berkley Corporation (NYSE: WRB) has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of 9 cents per share. This dividend will be paid on March 4, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 23, 2026. The company, founded in 1967, is a major commercial lines writer in the United States.

feed.title: Western Digital execs to speak at Morgan Stanley investor event
feed.summary: Western Digital Corporation announced that its management will participate in the upcoming Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on Tuesday, March 3, 2026. A live webcast of the presentation will be available on WD’s Investor Relations website, with an archived replay accessible afterward. Western Digital aims to highlight its role in the AI-driven data economy, providing storage infrastructure and innovative platforms.

feed.title: AMGEN INC SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Amgen Inc. has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing a year of significant financial growth with total revenues of $36,751 million and a diluted EPS of $14.23, an 88% increase. The report highlights strategic advancements, including new product launches and expanded manufacturing capacity, alongside challenges like regulatory risks and competition from biosimilars. Amgen plans continued investment in R&D and manufacturing while managing capital and shareholder returns.

feed.title: Uber Technologies, Inc SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Uber Technologies, Inc. has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing a revenue of $52,017 million and a substantial increase in income from operations. The report outlines significant growth in Mobility and Delivery segments, driven by increased trip volumes and strategic initiatives like Uber Direct and the Uber One membership program. Key challenges include driver classification issues, intense market competition, cybersecurity risks, and the inherent risks of international market operations and new technology investments.

feed.title: Tesla Leadership Shakeup Highlights Higher AI And Robotaxi Execution Risks
feed.summary: Tesla is undergoing executive changes, with Raj Jegannathan exiting and Joe Ward appointed as global head of sales, as the company intensifies its focus on AI, robotaxis, and the Optimus humanoid robot. These shifts aim to reposition Tesla as a broader technology platform, despite cooling demand for its core EV business and heightened execution risks associated with its ambitious new projects. Investors are advised to monitor leadership changes, capital expenditure, and progress on robotaxi and Optimus initiatives amidst a pivot towards software, services, and robotics.

feed.title: Leidos to pay shareholders $0.43 per share on March 31, 2026
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings, Inc. has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.43 per share, payable on March 31, 2026. Shareholders of record by the close of business on March 16, 2026, will be eligible to receive this dividend. The announcement was made on February 13, 2026, by the company's board of directors.

feed.title: Humana unit buys Florida clinic network serving 120,000 patients
feed.summary: Humana's CenterWell healthcare services division has completed its acquisition of MaxHealth, a Florida-based primary care organization. The acquisition expands CenterWell Senior Primary Care's reach with MaxHealth's network of 82 clinics serving over 120,000 patients, including 80,000 in value-based care programs. This move aims to provide personalized and integrated care to more patients in new key markets.

feed.title: Honeywell to pay $1.19-a-share dividend in March payout
feed.summary: Honeywell (NASDAQ: HON) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.19 per share, declared by its Board of Directors on February 13, 2026. This dividend is payable on March 13, 2026, to shareholders of record as of the close of business on February 27, 2026, and will be paid out of surplus. The company also provided details on its operations as an integrated operating company serving various industries globally.

feed.title: Travere Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TVTX) Stock Price Down 7.9% - Should You Sell?
feed.summary: Travere Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TVTX) saw its stock price drop by 7.9% on Friday, trading below its 50-day simple moving average. Despite this decline, analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $38.62. Insider selling has occurred with approximately 4.19% ownership remaining, while institutional investors have adjusted their positions.

feed.title: NVIDIA vs. SMCI: Which AI Hardware Stock Is the Better Buy Now?
feed.summary: This article compares NVIDIA (NVDA) and Super Micro Computer, Inc. (SMCI) as AI hardware stock investments. While both companies benefit from the AI infrastructure boom, NVIDIA is presented as the stronger buy due to its robust profitability, higher gross margins, and lower financial risk compared to Supermicro, which faces margin pressure despite strong revenue growth. NVIDIA holds a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy), while Supermicro has a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold).
